matches.match_rationale: Government funding cash flows follow predictable seasonal cycles. Historical claim-to-cash delay patterns can be modeled to produce campus-level cash arrival forecasts, giving the CFO a 30-90 day forward view of liquidity gaps.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A cash flow forecasting dashboard that ingests historical government funding receipt dates, claim submission logs, and fixed cost schedules to predict month-by-month liquidity shortfalls per campus.

matches.match_rationale: The claim-to-cash cycle involves repeated multi-step workflows: preparing claim documentation, submitting to government portals, tracking approval status, following up on partial releases, and reconciling receipts.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An agent that monitors government funding portals on a schedule, parses approval/rejection notices, auto-prepares re-submission packages, and sends the CFO a daily digest of claim statuses.

matches.match_rationale: Government funding claim processes often involve PDF remittance advices, scanned approval letters, and portal-exported reports that must be manually read and reconciled.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An OCR and extraction pipeline that ingests government remittance PDFs and approval letters, extracts structured fields, and feeds them directly into the accounting system.

matches.match_rationale: When partial fund releases require re-submission or follow-up correspondence with government agencies, drafting accurate, policy-compliant letters is time-consuming.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A tool that generates a draft re-submission letter or escalation memo from structured record data — reviewed and sent by the finance officer, but drafted in seconds.
